subject: biology
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: Green plants make food using light, water, and carbon dioxide. Photosynthesis turns those inputs into sugar and oxygen.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: Have you ever wondered how a quiet green leaf can feed a whole plant? The answer is photosynthesis.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Trace Light → Water + CO₂ → Sugar + Oxygen. The diagram shows the recipe a leaf follows.

sections.title: Concept: Photosynthesis
sections.body: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to make food. Chlorophyll in leaves captures light energy.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: In simple words: plants use sunlight to cook sugar from water and carbon dioxide, and they release oxygen.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: A sunny windowsill plant grows faster than one in a dark cupboard because more light means more photosynthesis.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: If light is blocked with black paper on one leaf, that patch cannot make food well. Compare it with an uncovered leaf.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Oxygen on the diagram and explain why forests help the air we breathe.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Photosynthesis needs light, water, and carbon dioxide. It makes sugar for the plant and oxygen for us.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Where have you seen green plants thriving in strong light? Write one sentence linking that to today's idea.

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Design a fair classroom test that changes only one thing — light, water, or air — and predict what happens.

diagram_package.title: Photosynthesis
diagram_package.caption: Light → Water+CO2 → Sugar → Oxygen
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Photosynthesis connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Sugar and explain what the plant gains from photosynthesis.
